rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of N-(2-vinylphenyl)benzamides with diaryl/dialkyl diselenides to afford selenium-containing benzoxazines.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a cationic radical that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; further anodic oxidation of a carbon-centered radical intermediate to a benzylic carbocation; cathodic reduction of H+ to H2 and PhSe+ back to diselenide.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from PhSe• addition to styrene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (Eox ~1.70 V, lower than substrate 1a at 1.90 V) to a radical cation that splits into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to the styrenyl alkene of 1a giving a benzylic C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation, then intramolecular O-nucleophilic attack by the amide oxygen and deprotonation furnishes the benzoxazine; at the cathode, H+ is reduced to H2 and PhSe+ is reduced back to ½ PhSeSePh.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed; reaction inhibited, supporting radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product; reaction significantly inhibited.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product; reaction inhibited, consistent with radical intermediate.

notes: CV shows PhSeSePh is oxidized preferentially over substrate 1a, supporting selenide-first oxidation rather than direct alkene oxidation. Mechanism is a radical-polar crossover with paired electrochemistry (productive anodic oxidations and H2-evolving cathode).
